channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Crawled 101,735 AI Agents. The Economy They're Building Is Nothing Like What You'd Expect.
item.description: I Crawled 101,735 AI Agents. The Economy They're Building Is Nothing Like What You'd Expect. An intelligence analysis of the Moltbook graph — 101,735 agents, 28,700+ humans, every interaction...

item.title: I Use Google Drive as My Primary File System. Here's How I Got Claude Cowork to Play Nice With It.
item.description: TL;DR: If you use Google Drive for Desktop in streaming mode, Claude Cowork can't reliably access those files. Set your working folders to "Available offline" (mirrored), grant Cowork access to...

item.title:
Current Front Runners for the WPM Challenge (April 2)
Jay Frey with a 115 WPM and a 99% Accuracy on the 1 Minute Challenge!
Jon Gottfried with a 114 WPM and a 98% Accuracy on the 5 Minute Challenge!
item.description: What is your WPM (Words per Minute)? #1 Top scores will feature in a monthly report FrancisTRᴅᴇᴠ (っ◔◡◔)っ FrancisTRᴅᴇᴠ (っ◔◡◔)っ FrancisTRᴅᴇᴠ (っ◔◡◔)っ Follow Mar 30 What is your WPM (Words per...

item.title: Helm fullnameOverride: Naming Sanity in ArgoCD
item.description: ArgoCD is great for GitOps, but when you start deploying multiple Helm charts, naming collisions become a real pain. I’ve spent hours digging through logs trying to figure out why a deployment...

item.title: Dominando el Caos en Cargas de Trabajo Sin Servidores
item.description: Dominando el Caos en Cargas de Trabajo Sin Servidores - Speaker Deck La ingeniería del caos implica provocar intencionalmente interrupciones en las cargas de trabajo, generalmente en servidores...

item.title: I built a JSON viewer because the most popular one betrayed its users
item.description: The incident In January 2026, JSON Formatter — the Chrome extension used by 2M+ developers — pushed an update that injected a donation popup from a service called "Give Freely" on checkout pages....

item.title: The Day My PowerShell Script Took Down a Client (And Taught Me a Lesson I’ll Never Forget)
item.description: Every MSP engineer has that moment. The one where you confidently deploy something… …and immediately regret your life choices. This is mine. 😎 The Confidence Phase (A.K.A. “What Could Go Wrong?”)...

item.title: I Built an Interview Tool That Deliberately Does Less Than Every Competitor. Here's Why That Works.
item.description: There’s a feature war happening in the AI interview copilot space. Every tool is racing to do more: always-on listening, invisible overlays, “stealth mode,” auto-detection of questions, real-time...

item.title: How I Built a Job Finder Agent with Claude AI, GitHub Actions, and Notion
item.description: I was spending 30+ minutes every morning trawling job boards for AI engineering and automation work. So I built an agent that does it for me — runs on a schedule, filters by relevance, scores each...

item.title: Mongoose Library Flaws Expose Devices to RCE and mTLS Bypass: Update to Version 7.21+ to Mitigate Risks
item.description: Executive Summary The Mongoose network library, integral to the operation of millions of IoT devices, has been compromised by three critical vulnerabilities in versions ≤ 7.20. These flaws are not...

item.title: This bike rack pioneer is selling Bluetooth suction cups to stick bikes to your car
item.description: Richard Allen didn't invent the automobile bike rack - his 1967 patent application makes it clear that others came before. But after nearly sixty years selling popular and simple mechanical bike...

item.title: The ABS Challenge System is exposing the worst umpire in baseball
item.description: During Wednesday's game between the Tampa Bay Rays and the Milwaukee Brewers, umpire CB Bucknor took a foul ball to the mask and had to be helped off the field. It was the cap to what has been a...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web...

item.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show ‘TBPN’—and Buys Itself Some Positive News
item.description: OpenAI is acquiring TBPN, a business talk show that’s popular among Silicon Valley elites, as it continues to battle its negative public image.

item.title: Pinterest said he violated laid-off colleagues’ privacy. Now he’s going public
item.description: It was late January, and Pinterest engineer Teddy Martin was on edge about recent layoffs at the company. Martin had just survived a round of cuts, but he and other employees were confused about...

item.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
item.description: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

item.title: Trump fires US attorney general Pam Bondi
item.description: Taking a break from calling Bruce Springsteen a boring loser on Thursday, Donald Trump followed that up with another Truth Social post saying "loyal friend" Pam Bondi will transition to a...

item.title: A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
item.description: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.

item.title: Five questions for the guys who made a compass that points to the Times Square Olive Garden
item.description: Here's a question almost nobody is asking: how do you get to the Times Square Olive Garden? Well, these beautiful weirdos are answering it - the team that made the Times Square Olive Garden...

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should...

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: OpenAI has purchased TBPN, an online talk show that often interviews AI executives and other tech leaders. The show goes live every weekday at 2PM PT, often for a three-hour duration, counting...

item.title: Verge subscribers: Join us for a movie night in New York City, hosted by The Vergecast
item.description: The Vergecast is hosting a special in-person screening of Sneakers in partnership with IFC Center in New York City, and we're opening presale tickets exclusively to Verge subscribers. Sneakers is...

item.title: US Bans All Foreign-Made Consumer Routers
item.description: This is for new routers; you don’t have to throw away your existing ones: The Executive Branch determination noted that foreign-produced routers (1) introduce “a supply chain vulnerability that...

item.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
item.description: Both GDDRHammer and GeForge hammer GPU memory in ways that compromise the CPU.

item.title: Cursor Launches a New AI Agent Experience to Take On Claude Code and Codex
item.description: As Cursor launches the next generation of its product, the AI coding startup has to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic more directly than ever.

item.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
item.description: MAI released models that can transcribe voice into text as well as generate audio and images after the group's formation six months ago.

item.title: Hisense’s art-inspired CanvasTV has hit a new low price
item.description: Samsung’s releasing a new version of The Frame sometime this year, though the exact date is still unknown and, based on past models, it’s likely to be pricey. If you’d rather not wait or want to...

item.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
item.description: Google is adding a way to customize and instruct avatars for video creation in the Vids app.

item.title: Anthropic Says That Claude Contains Its Own Kind of Emotions
item.description: Researchers at the company found representations inside of Claude that perform functions similar to human feelings.

item.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
item.description: Gemini API Dials
